name: Qasimi Imamate (Zaidi)
description: The longest Yemeni dynasty — lasting 365 years — and the one whose architectural patronage is most directly visible in the existing built fabric of highland Yemen. The Qasimi Imams refined the tower house typology, brought the zabur rammed-earth tradition to its greatest height in Sa'dah, and maintained Sana'a's urban character. The Imamate ended with the 1962 revolution.
